content_en:
al-Jannah wa an-Naar – In the Light of the Qur'aan and Sunnah

changes; rivers of wine delicious to those who drink; and rivers of clarified honey [clear and pure].." [47:15] Al-Tirmidhi reports, with a sahih isnaad, from Haakim ibn Mu'aawiyah that the Messenger of Allaah (saw) said, "In Paradise there is a sea of honey, a sea of wine, a sea of milk and a sea of water, and the rivers flow out of these seas." [Jaami' al-Usool, 10/508] He also told us of a river called Baariq, which flows by the gate of Paradise. During the period of al-Barzakh [the time between death and the day of Judgement] the shuhadaa are beside this river: Ibn 'Abbaas narrated that the Messenger of Allaah (saw) said, "The shuhadaa [martyrs] are in a green dome beside the river of Baariq, near the gate of Paradise, from which provision comes to them morning and evening." [sahih al-Jaami' as-Sagheer, 3/235, no. 3636]

The Springs of Paradise In Paradise there are many springs that provide drinks of different tastes: "Truly al-Muttaqoon [the pious and righteous] will be amidst Gardens and Water-springs" [15:45] "Verily al-Muttaqoon shall be amidst shades and springs." [77:41] Concerning the two Gardens which Allaah (swt) has prepared for those who fear their Rabb, Allaah (swt) said, "In them [both] will be two springs flowing [free]." [55:50] And concerning the two Gardens beneath them, He said, "In them [both] will be two springs gushing forth water." [55:66] In Paradise there are two springs from which al-Muqarraboon will drink pure and undiluted, whilst the Abraar will drink their water mixed with something else. The first is the spring of Kafoor, as Allaah (swt) says: "Verily the Abraar [the pious, those who fear Allaah and avoid evil], shall drink a cup [of wine] mixed with water from a spring in Paradise called Kafoor, A spring wherefrom the slaves of Allaah will drink, causing it to gush forth abundantly." [76:5-6] He tells us that the pious will drink from it mixed with something else, whilst those close to Allaah will drink it pure and undiluted. The second spring is of Tasneem, as Allaah (swt) says: "Verily al-Abraar will be in delight [Paradise], On thrones, looking [at all things]. You will recognise in their faces the brightness of delight. They will b given to drink pure sealed wine, the last thereof [that wine] will be the smell of musk, and for this let [all] those who strive who want to strive [i.e. hasten earnestly to the obedience of Allaah]. It [that wine] will be mixed with Tasneem, a spring whereof drink those nearest to Allaah." [83:22-27] Another of the springs of Paradise is called al-Salsabeel as Allaah (swt) says: "And they will be given to drink there a cup [of wine] mixed with zanjabeel [ginger], a spring there, called Salsabeel". [76:17-18]

al-Jannah wa an-Naar – In the Light of the Qur'aan and Sunnah

This is probably the same spring as that mentioned above, i.e. Kafoor.

The Palaces and Tents of Paradise Allaah (swt) will build good and beautiful dwellings for the people of Paradise: "and beautiful mansions in Gardens of everlasting bliss" [9:72] In some places in the Qur'aan, Allaah (swt) described these dwellings as ghurafaat [chambers or dwellings]: "and they will reside in the high dwellings [Paradise] in peace and security." [34:37] The reward for the slaves of the all-Merciful will be: "Those will be rewarded with the highest place [in Paradise] because of their patience. Therein they shall be met with greetings and the word of peace and respect" [25:75] Allaah (swt) described these Ghurafaat: "But for those who fear Allaah and keep their duty to their Rabb [Allaah], for them are built lofty rooms, one above the other, under which rivers flow. [This is] the Promise of Allaah, and Allaah does not fail in [His] Promise." [39:20] Ibn Katheer said: "Allaah has told us about His blessed slaves who will have rooms [or dwellings] in Paradise: these will be lofty palaces, "lofty rooms, one above another - story upon story, well-constructed, strong and decorated. The Prophet (saw) described these palaces to us. According to a hadith narrated by Ahmad, and Ibn Hibbaan from Abu Maalik al-Ash'ari and by Al-Tirmidhi from 'Ali (ra) the Prophet (saw) said: "In Paradise there are dwellings whose inside can be seen from the outside, and the outside can be seen from inside. Allaah [swt] has prepared them for those who feed the hungry, and speak softly and gently, fast continuously and pray at night whilst the people are asleep." [sahih alJaami' as-Sagheer, 2/220, no. 2119] Allaah (swt) has told us that there are tents or pavilions in Paradise: "Houris [beautiful, fair females] restrained in pavilions" [55:72] These pavilions are wondrous tents, made of pearls, each one is made from a single, hollowed-out pearl. They are sixty miles high; according to some reports they are sixty miles wide. al-Bukhaari reports from 'Abdullaah ibn Qays that the Messenger of Allaah (saw) said, "The tent is a hollowed out pearl, thirty miles high; in each corner of it the believer will have a wife whom no one else can see". Abu 'Abdus-Samad and al-Haarith reported from Abu 'Imraan that the wording was "sixty miles high" [Sahih alBukhaari, Kitaab Bid' al-Khalq, Baab Sifaat al-Jannah, Fath al-Baari, 6/318] Muslim reported from 'Abdullaah ibn Qays that the Prophet (saw) said: "The believer in Paradise will have a tent made out of a single, hollowed-out pearl, sixty miles long, in which he will have [a number of] wives, whom he will visit in turn, none of whom will see the others". [see also Muslim, Kitaab al-Jannah, Baab fi Sifaat Khiyaam al-Jannah, 4/2182, no. 2838]

al-Jannah wa an-Naar – In the Light of the Qur'aan and Sunnah

The Prophet (saw) told us about the characteristics of the palaces of some of his wives and companions. alBukhaari and Muslim report that Abu Hurayrah (ra) said, "Jibreel came to the Prophet (saw) said, "O Messenger of Allaah, Khadeejah is coming, carrying a container of food. When she comes to you, convey to her greetings of peace from her Rabb and from me, and give her the glad tidings of a house in Paradise made of brocade, in which there is no noise or exhaustion."" [Mishkaat alMasaabeeh, 3/266] al-Bukhaari and Muslim report from Jaabir that the Messenger of Allaah (saw) said, "I entered Paradise, where I saw ar-Rumaysa', the wife of Abu Talhah. And I heard footsteps and asked, "Who is that?" He [Jibreel] said, "That is Bilaal." And I saw a palace with women in its courtyard. I asked, "Whose is this?" They said, "It is for 'Umar ibn al-Khattab". I had wanted to go in and look at it, but I remembered your jealousy [where women are concerned]." 'Umar said, "May my mother and father be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allaah (saw), would I feel jealous from you?" [Mishkaat alMasaabeeh, 3/226] The Prophet (saw) has told us of the way in which the believer may acquire more than one house in Paradise: Ahmad reports with a sahih isnaad from Ibn 'Abbaas that the Prophet (saw) said, "Whoever builds a masjid for Allaah, even if it is as small as the nestling-place scratched out by the sane grouse for its eggs, Allaah (swt) will build a house for him in Paradise". [sahih al-Jaami' as-Sagheer, 5/265, no. 6005] Ahmad, al-Bukhaari, Muslim, Al-Tirmidhi and Ibn Maajah report from 'Uthmaan that the Messenger of Allaah (saw) said "Whoever builds a mosque for Allaah (swt), Allaah (swt) will build something similar for him in Paradise". [sahih al-Jaami' as-Sagheer, 5/265, no. 6007] Muslim, Abu Dawud, Ahmad, an-Nasaa'ee, and Ibn Maajah report from Umm Habeebah that the Messenger of Allaah (saw) said, "Whoever prays twelve extra [supererogatory] rak'ahs every day, Allaah (swt) will build for him a house in Paradise." [sahih al-Jaami', 5/316, no. 6234]

The Light of Paradise al-Qurtubee said, "The scholars said there is no night and day in Paradise, rather they will be in eternal everlasting light. They will know when night comes because curtains or screens will be put up and doors will be closed, and they will know when day comes because the curtains or screens will be taken down and the doors will be opened. This was mentioned by Abul-Faraj ibn al-Jawzee" [al-Qurtubee, at-Tadhkirah, p. 504] Ibn Katheer in his commentary on the aayah: "And they will have therein their sustenance, morning and evening. Such is the Paradise, which We shall give as an inheritance to those of Our slave who have been al-Muttaqoon [pious and righteous]" [19:62-63] said, "This means something approximate to the times of day and night, it does not mean that there will be a day and night there. They will know the passing of time by the changes in the light" [Tafseer Ibn Katheer, 4/471] On the same subject, Ibn Taymiyyah said, "There is no sun and moon in Paradise, and no day or night, but they will know morning and evening from a light that shines from the direction of the Throne" [Majmu' Fataawa Shaikh al-Islaam, 4/312]

al-Jannah wa an-Naar – In the Light of the Qur'aan and Sunnah

Paradise is filled with a pure and beautiful fragrance, which the believers will be able to discern from a great distance. Ahmad, an-Nasaa'ee, Ibn Maajah and al-Haakim report with a sahih isnaad that the Messenger (saw) said, "Whoever kills a man of Ahl ad-Dhimmah [non-Muslims living under Islaamic rule] will not smell the fragrance of Paradise, even though its fragrance can be discerned from a distance of forty years' travelling." [sahih al-Jaami as-Sagheer, 5/235, no. 6324 adn 5/337 no. 6333]

The Trees and Fruits of Paradise (i) Its trees and fruits are of many kinds and everlasting The trees of Paradise are abundant and good and of many kinds. Allaah has told us that there are grapevines, datepalms and pomegranate trees in Paradise, as well as lotus trees and acacia trees: "Verily, for the muttaqoon, there will be a success [Paradise], gardens and grape yards" [78:3132] "in them [both will be fruits, date palms and pomegranates" [55:68] "And those on the Right Hand - who will be those on the Right Hand? [They will be] among thornless lote-trees, Among Talh trees with fruits piled one above another, In shade longextended, By water flowing constantly, And fruit in plenty" [56:27-32] The lotus tree [ad-Sidr] is a thorny plant, but in Paradise it will be thornless. at-Talh [acacia tree] is a tree of Hijaaz, a kind of 'idah [a fair sized thorny shrub], but in Paradise its fruits will be ready to eat, with no effort required. What Allaah (swt) has told us about the trees of Paradise is only a small part of what Paradise contains. Hence Allaah told us "In them [both] will be every kind of fruit in pairs" [55:52] Because of its abundance, people will be able to leave what they want and take what they want: "...and therein they will call for fruits in abundance and drinks". [38:51] "With fruits, any that they may select" [56:20] "Verily the Muttaqoon shall be amidst shades and springs, And fruits - such as they desire" [77:4142] In short, Paradise contains all kinds of fruits and other joys that one's heart could desire: "Trays of gold and cups will be passed round them, [there will be] therein all that one's soul could desire, all that the eyes could delight in, and you will abide therein forever" [43:71] Ibn Katheer spoke very cleverly about the greatness of the fruits of Paradise, and concluded that by means of understatement, Allaah (swt) conveyed the abundance and greatness of those fruits. He said, "The lotus tree [as-Sidr] does not bear any fruit other than some insignificant, inferior fruits, and it is thorny. The acacia tree [at-Talh] is only used for shade in this world [not for fruit] but in Paradise there will be many of them,
content_id:
al-Jannah wa an-Naar – Dalam Cahaya al-Qur'an dan Sunnah

berubah; sungai-sungai berisi khamar yang lezat bagi yang meminumnya; dan sungai-sungai berisi madu murni yang jernih dan bersih.." [47:15] Al-Tirmidzi meriwayatkan dengan isnad yang shahih dari Hakim ibn Mu'awiyah bahwa Rasulullah (saw) bersabda, "Di surga terdapat lautan madu, lautan khamar, lautan susu, dan lautan air, dan sungai-sungai mengalir dari lautan-lautan ini." [Jaami' al-Usool, 10/508] Beliau juga memberitahukan kepada kita tentang sebuah sungai bernama Baariq, yang mengalir di dekat pintu surga. Selama masa al-Barzakh [masa antara kematian dan hari Kiamat] para syuhada berada di tepi sungai ini: Ibn 'Abbas meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (saw) bersabda, "Para syuhada [orang-orang yang mati syahid] berada di bawah kubah hijau di tepi sungai Baariq, dekat pintu surga, dari mana mereka mendapat rezeki pagi dan petang." [Shahih al-Jaami' ash-Shagheer, 3/235, no. 3636]

Mata Air Surga Di surga terdapat banyak mata air yang mengalirkan minuman dengan berbagai rasa: "Sesungguhnya al-Muttaqoon [orang-orang yang bertakwa dan saleh] akan berada di tengah taman-taman dan mata air-mata air" [15:45] "Sesungguhnya al-Muttaqoon akan berada di tengah naungan-naungan dan mata air-mata air." [77:41] Mengenai dua taman yang Allah (swt) telah persiapkan bagi orang-orang yang takut kepada Rabb mereka, Allah (swt) berfirman, "Di dalamnya [keduanya] terdapat dua mata air yang mengalir [bebas]." [55:50] Dan mengenai dua taman di bawahnya, Dia berfirman, "Di dalamnya [keduanya] terdapat dua mata air yang memancarkan air." [55:66] Di surga terdapat dua mata air yang darinya al-Muqarraboon akan minum dalam keadaan murni dan tidak tercampur, sementara al-Abraar akan meminum airnya yang tercampur dengan sesuatu yang lain. Yang pertama adalah mata air Kafoor, sebagaimana Allah (swt) berfirman: "Sesungguhnya al-Abraar [orang-orang yang saleh, mereka yang takut kepada Allah dan menjauhi kejahatan] akan meminum cangkir [khamar] yang dicampur dengan air dari sebuah mata air di surga bernama Kafoor, sebuah mata air dari mana para hamba Allah akan minum, yang mengalirkannya dengan deras." [76:5-6] Dia memberitahukan kepada kita bahwa orang-orang yang saleh akan meminumnya dalam keadaan tercampur dengan sesuatu yang lain, sementara mereka yang dekat dengan Allah akan meminumnya dalam keadaan murni dan tidak tercampur. Yang kedua adalah mata air Tasneem, sebagaimana Allah (swt) berfirman: "Sesungguhnya al-Abraar akan berada dalam kenikmatan [surga], Di atas singgasana, memandang [segala sesuatu]. Engkau akan mengenali pada wajah mereka cahaya kenikmatan. Mereka akan diberi minum khamar murni yang tersegel, yang akhirnya [khamar itu] berbau kesturi, dan untuk inilah hendaknya [semua] orang yang ingin bersungguh-sungguh bersungguh-sungguh [yakni bersegera dengan sepenuh hati dalam ketaatan kepada Allah]. Khamar itu dicampur dengan Tasneem, sebuah mata air yang diminum oleh mereka yang paling dekat kepada Allah." [83:22-27] Mata air surga lainnya bernama al-Salsabeel sebagaimana Allah (swt) berfirman: "Dan di sana mereka akan diberi minum cangkir [khamar] yang dicampur dengan zanjabeel [jahe], sebuah mata air di sana, bernama Salsabeel". [76:17-18]

al-Jannah wa an-Naar – Dalam Cahaya al-Qur'an dan Sunnah

Ini mungkin merupakan mata air yang sama dengan yang disebutkan di atas, yaitu Kafoor.

Istana dan Kemah-Kemah Surga Allah (swt) akan membangun tempat tinggal yang baik dan indah bagi penghuni surga: "dan tempat-tempat tinggal yang indah di taman-taman kebahagiaan yang kekal" [9:72] Di beberapa tempat dalam al-Qur'an, Allah (swt) menggambarkan tempat-tempat tinggal ini sebagai ghurafaat [kamar-kamar atau kediaman]: "dan mereka akan tinggal di kediaman yang tinggi [surga] dalam kedamaian dan keamanan." [34:37] Ganjaran bagi para hamba ar-Rahman adalah: "Mereka itulah yang akan dibalas dengan tempat yang paling tinggi [di surga] karena kesabaran mereka. Di sana mereka akan disambut dengan ucapan salam dan kata-kata penghormatan" [25:75] Allah (swt) menggambarkan Ghurafaat ini: "Tetapi bagi mereka yang takut kepada Allah dan menunaikan kewajiban mereka kepada Rabb mereka [Allah], bagi mereka dibangun kamar-kamar yang tinggi, satu di atas yang lainnya, di bawahnya mengalir sungai-sungai. [Ini adalah] Janji Allah, dan Allah tidak pernah mengingkari [Janji-Nya]." [39:20] Ibn Katsir berkata: "Allah telah memberitahukan kepada kita tentang para hamba-Nya yang diberkati yang akan memiliki kamar-kamar [atau kediaman] di surga: ini adalah istana-istana yang megah, 'kamar-kamar yang tinggi, satu di atas yang lainnya - tingkat demi tingkat, dibangun dengan baik, kuat dan dihiasi.' Nabi (saw) menggambarkan istana-istana ini kepada kita. Berdasarkan sebuah hadits yang diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, dan Ibn Hibban dari Abu Malik al-Asy'ari dan oleh Al-Tirmidzi dari 'Ali (ra), Nabi (saw) bersabda: "Di surga terdapat kediaman yang bagian dalamnya dapat dilihat dari luar, dan bagian luarnya dapat dilihat dari dalam. Allah [swt] telah mempersiapkannya bagi mereka yang memberi makan orang yang lapar, berbicara dengan lembut dan santun, berpuasa secara terus-menerus, dan shalat di malam hari sementara orang-orang tidur." [Shahih al-Jaami' ash-Shagheer, 2/220, no. 2119] Allah (swt) telah memberitahukan kepada kita bahwa di surga terdapat kemah-kemah atau paviliun: "Bidadari-bidadari [perempuan yang cantik dan rupawan] yang tinggal di dalam paviliun" [55:72] Paviliun-paviliun ini adalah kemah-kemah yang menakjubkan, terbuat dari mutiara, masing-masing dibuat dari satu mutiara yang dilubangi. Tingginya enam puluh mil; menurut beberapa riwayat lebarnya enam puluh mil. Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Qays bahwa Rasulullah (saw) bersabda, "Kemah itu adalah mutiara yang dilubangi, setinggi tiga puluh mil; di setiap sudutnya orang beriman akan memiliki seorang istri yang tidak dapat dilihat oleh siapa pun." Abu 'Abdus-Samad dan al-Harits meriwayatkan dari Abu 'Imran bahwa redaksinya adalah "setinggi enam puluh mil" [Shahih al-Bukhari, Kitaab Bid' al-Khalq, Baab Shifaat al-Jannah, Fath al-Baari, 6/318] Muslim meriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Qays bahwa Nabi (saw) bersabda: "Orang beriman di surga akan memiliki kemah yang terbuat dari satu mutiara yang dilubangi, panjangnya enam puluh mil, di dalamnya ia akan memiliki [sejumlah] istri yang akan ia kunjungi secara bergantian, yang satu tidak dapat melihat yang lainnya". [lihat juga Muslim, Kitaab al-Jannah, Baab fi Shifaat Khiyaam al-Jannah, 4/2182, no. 2838]

al-Jannah wa an-Naar – Dalam Cahaya al-Qur'an dan Sunnah

Nabi (saw) memberitahukan kepada kita tentang ciri-ciri istana beberapa istri dan sahabat beliau. Al-Bukhari dan Muslim meriwayatkan bahwa Abu Hurairah (ra) berkata, "Jibril datang kepada Nabi (saw) dan berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, Khadijah datang membawa wadah berisi makanan. Ketika ia datang kepadamu, sampaikan kepadanya salam dari Rabb-nya dan dariku, dan berilah ia kabar gembira tentang sebuah rumah di surga yang terbuat dari sutra brokad, di dalamnya tidak ada kebisingan maupun kelelahan.'" [Misykaat al-Masaabeeh, 3/266] Al-Bukhari dan Muslim meriwayatkan dari Jabir bahwa Rasulullah (saw) bersabda, "Aku memasuki surga, di mana aku melihat ar-Rumaysa', istri Abu Thalhah. Dan aku mendengar suara langkah kaki lalu bertanya, 'Siapakah itu?' Dia [Jibril] menjawab, 'Itu adalah Bilal.' Dan aku melihat sebuah istana dengan para wanita di halamannya. Aku bertanya, 'Milik siapakah ini?' Mereka menjawab, 'Ini milik 'Umar ibn al-Khatthab'. Aku ingin masuk dan melihatnya, tetapi aku teringat kecemburuanmu [dalam hal wanita]." 'Umar berkata, "Demi bapak dan ibuku sebagai tebusan untukmu, wahai Rasulullah (saw), apakah aku akan merasa cemburu kepadamu?" [Misykaat al-Masaabeeh, 3/226] Nabi (saw) telah memberitahukan kepada kita tentang cara seorang mukmin dapat memperoleh lebih dari satu rumah di surga: Ahmad meriwayatkan dengan isnad yang shahih dari Ibn 'Abbas bahwa Nabi (saw) bersabda, "Barang siapa membangun masjid karena Allah, meskipun hanya sekecil tempat bertelur burung qataa, Allah (swt) akan membangun sebuah rumah untuknya di surga." [Shahih al-Jaami' ash-Shagheer, 5/265, no. 6005] Ahmad, al-Bukhari, Muslim, Al-Tirmidzi dan Ibn Majah meriwayatkan dari 'Utsman bahwa Rasulullah (saw) bersabda, "Barang siapa membangun masjid karena Allah (swt), Allah (swt) akan membangun sesuatu yang serupa untuknya di surga." [Shahih al-Jaami' ash-Shagheer, 5/265, no. 6007] Muslim, Abu Dawud, Ahmad, an-Nasai, dan Ibn Majah meriwayatkan dari Ummu Habibah bahwa Rasulullah (saw) bersabda, "Barang siapa mengerjakan dua belas rakaat shalat sunnah setiap hari, Allah (swt) akan membangun untuknya sebuah rumah di surga." [Shahih al-Jaami', 5/316, no. 6234]

Cahaya Surga Al-Qurthubi berkata, "Para ulama mengatakan tidak ada malam dan siang di surga, melainkan mereka akan berada dalam cahaya yang kekal dan abadi. Mereka akan mengetahui ketika malam tiba karena tirai atau tabir akan dipasang dan pintu-pintu akan ditutup, dan mereka akan mengetahui ketika siang tiba karena tirai atau tabir akan dibuka dan pintu-pintu akan dibuka. Hal ini disebutkan oleh Abul-Faraj ibn al-Jawzi" [al-Qurthubi, at-Tadzkirah, h. 504] Ibn Katsir dalam tafsirnya tentang ayat: "Dan mereka akan mendapat rezeki pagi dan petang di dalamnya. Itulah surga yang akan Kami wariskan kepada hamba-hamba Kami yang bertakwa" [19:62-63] berkata, "Ini berarti sesuatu yang mendekati waktu siang dan malam, bukan berarti akan ada siang dan malam di sana. Mereka akan mengetahui berlalunya waktu dari perubahan cahaya." [Tafseer Ibn Katsir, 4/471] Mengenai hal yang sama, Ibn Taimiyyah berkata, "Tidak ada matahari dan bulan di surga, dan tidak ada siang atau malam, tetapi mereka akan mengetahui waktu pagi dan petang dari cahaya yang bersinar dari arah 'Arsy." [Majmu' Fatawa Syaikh al-Islam, 4/312]

al-Jannah wa an-Naar – Dalam Cahaya Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah

Surga dipenuhi dengan wewangian yang murni dan indah, yang dapat dirasakan oleh orang-orang beriman dari jarak yang sangat jauh. Ahmad, an-Nasaa'ee, Ibn Maajah, dan al-Haakim meriwayatkan dengan sanad yang sahih bahwa Rasulullah (saw) bersabda, "Barang siapa membunuh seorang laki-laki dari Ahl adh-Dhimmah [non-Muslim yang hidup di bawah pemerintahan Islam], maka ia tidak akan mencium wewangian surga, padahal wewanginya dapat dirasakan dari jarak perjalanan empat puluh tahun." [Shahih al-Jaami ash-Shagheer, 5/235, no. 6324 dan 5/337 no. 6333]

Pohon-pohon dan Buah-buahan Surga (i) Pohon dan buahnya beraneka ragam dan kekal abadi. Pohon-pohon surga sangat banyak, indah, dan beraneka ragam jenisnya. Allah telah memberitahukan kepada kita bahwa di surga terdapat pohon anggur, pohon kurma, dan pohon delima, serta pohon bidara dan pohon akasia: "Sesungguhnya bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa, terdapat kemenangan [surga], kebun-kebun dan kebun anggur." [78:31-32] "Di dalam keduanya terdapat buah-buahan, pohon kurma dan pohon delima." [55:68] "Dan golongan kanan, siapakah golongan kanan itu? [Mereka berada] di antara pohon bidara yang tidak berduri, di antara pohon pisang yang bersusun-susun buahnya, dalam naungan yang terbentang luas, di sisi air yang mengalir terus-menerus, dan buah-buahan yang banyak." [56:27-32] Pohon bidara [as-Sidr] adalah tanaman berduri, namun di surga ia tidak berduri. at-Talh [pohon akasia] adalah pohon dari Hijaz, sejenis semak berduri berukuran sedang, namun di surga buahnya siap dimakan tanpa memerlukan usaha apa pun. Apa yang telah Allah (swt) beritahukan kepada kita tentang pohon-pohon surga hanyalah sebagian kecil dari apa yang terkandung di dalamnya. Oleh karena itu, Allah berfirman: "Di dalam keduanya terdapat segala jenis buah-buahan yang berpasangan." [55:52] Karena melimpahnya, orang-orang dapat meninggalkan apa yang tidak mereka inginkan dan mengambil apa yang mereka inginkan: "...dan di dalamnya mereka meminta buah-buahan yang melimpah dan minuman." [38:51] "Dengan buah-buahan pilihan sesuka mereka." [56:20] "Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang bertakwa berada di antara naungan-naungan dan mata air-mata air, serta buah-buahan sesuai keinginan mereka." [77:41-42] Singkatnya, surga mengandung segala jenis buah-buahan dan kenikmatan lainnya yang diidamkan oleh hati: "Kepada mereka diedarkan piring-piring dari emas dan piala-piala, dan di dalamnya terdapat segala yang diinginkan oleh jiwa dan yang menyenangkan pandangan mata, dan kamu kekal di dalamnya selama-lamanya." [43:71] Ibnu Katsir berbicara dengan sangat cermat mengenai keagungan buah-buahan surga, dan menyimpulkan bahwa melalui gaya bahasa yang merendah, Allah (swt) menyampaikan kelimpahan dan keagungan buah-buahan tersebut. Beliau berkata, "Pohon bidara [as-Sidr] tidak menghasilkan buah selain buah-buahan yang sepele dan rendah, serta berduri. Pohon akasia [at-Talh] di dunia hanya digunakan sebagai peneduh [bukan untuk buah], namun di surga jumlahnya sangat banyak,
title_id: Teks untuk diterjemahkan tidak disertakan dalam pesan Anda. Mohon sertakan konten bahasa Inggris yang ingin diterjemahkan pada Part 18.
content_ar: al-Jannah wa an-Naar – In the Light of the Qur'aan and السنة changes; rivers of wine delicious to those who drink; and rivers of clarified honey [clear and pure].." [47:15] Al-الترمذي reports, with a صحيح isnaad, from Haakim ibn Mu'aawiyah that الرسول ﷺ of Allaah (saw) said, "In الجنة there is a sea of honey, a sea of wine, a sea of milk and a sea of water, and the rivers flow out of these seas." [Jaami' al-Usool, 10/508] He أيضاً told us of a river called Baariq, which flows by the gate of الجنة. خلال the period of al-Barzakh [the time between الموت and the يوم القيامة] the shuhadaa are beside this river: ابن عباس narrated that الرسول ﷺ of Allaah (saw) said, "The shuhadaa [martyrs] are in a green dome beside the river of Baariq, near the gate of الجنة, from which provision comes to them morning and evening." [صحيح al-Jaami' as-Sagheer, 3/235, no. 3636] The Springs of الجنة In الجنة there are many springs that provide drinks of different tastes: "Truly al-Muttaqoon [the pious and الصالح] will be amidst Gardens and Water-springs" [15:45] "Verily al-Muttaqoon shall be amidst shades and springs." [77:41] فيما يتعلق بـ the two Gardens which Allaah (swt) has prepared for those who الخوف their Rabb, Allaah (swt) said, "In them [both] will be two springs flowing [free]." [55:50] And فيما يتعلق بـ the two Gardens beneath them, قال, "In them [both] will be two springs gushing forth water." [55:66] In الجنة there are two springs from which al-Muqarraboon will drink pure and undiluted, whilst the Abraar will drink their water mixed with something else. The أولاً is the spring of Kafoor, as Allaah (swt) says: "Verily the Abraar [the pious, those who الخوف Allaah and avoid الشر], shall drink a cup [of wine] mixed with water from a spring in الجنة called Kafoor, A spring wherefrom the slaves of Allaah will drink, causing it to gush forth abundantly." [76:5-6] He tells us that the pious will drink from it mixed with something else, whilst those close to Allaah will drink it pure and undiluted. The ثانياً spring is of Tasneem, as Allaah (swt) says: "Verily al-Abraar will be in delight [الجنة], On thrones, looking [at all things]. You will recognise in their faces the brightness of delight. They will b given to drink pure sealed wine, the last thereof [that wine] will be the smell of musk, and for this let [all] those who strive who want to strive [i.e. hasten earnestly to the obedience of Allaah]. It [that wine] will be mixed with Tasneem, a spring whereof drink those nearest to Allaah." [83:22-27] Another of the springs of الجنة is called al-Salsabeel as Allaah (swt) says: "And they will be given to drink there a cup [of wine] mixed with zanjabeel [ginger], a spring there, called Salsabeel". [76:17-18] al-Jannah wa an-Naar – In the Light of the Qur'aan and السنة This is probably the same spring as that mentioned above, i.e. Kafoor. The Palaces and Tents of الجنة Allaah (s
